**Q2. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023 Model]**
*Explain the role of the **Network Layer** in IoT healthcare with two examples of communication technologies used.*

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
> The Network Layer is responsible for transmitting digitized biomedical data from local sensors/gateways to remote cloud servers and clinical endpoints. It ensures reliable, low-latency, and secure delivery over heterogeneous links. **(1.5 marks)**
>
> Two communication technologies: **(1.5 marks)**
> 1. **Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11):** Used inside hospitals for high-bandwidth, real-time patient monitoring (e.g., 12-lead ECG streaming).
> 2. **NB-IoT / LTE-M:** Used for remote patient monitoring over cellular networks with extended coverage (e.g., elderly home monitoring).

---

### 📕 PART B — Full-Question Solutions (14 Marks with Internal Choice)

> **KTU Pattern:** Each Part B question has sub-parts (a) **7 marks** and (b) **7 marks**.

---

#### **Question A — 14 Marks** *(Choose either A or B)*

**[KTU University Exam — Sample 2024 Scheme]**
**(a) [7 Marks]** With a neat block diagram, explain the **three-layer architecture of IoT in healthcare**, mentioning the function and one example technology of each layer.

**(b) [7 Marks]** A wearable ECG patch samples at 400 Hz with 10-bit resolution and transmits via BLE (efficiency η = 0.5 bps/Hz, modulation = GFSK with M = 2). Calculate: (i) raw bit rate, (ii) bandwidth required, (iii) total power consumed if it draws 4 mA at 3 V, and (iv) battery life in days if battery capacity is 200 mAh.

**Model Solution (a):**

> **Block Diagram (3 marks):** Draw Perception Layer (sensors, electrodes) → Network Layer (BLE/Wi-Fi/5G) → Application Layer (cloud, analytics, clinician app). Use boxes with labeled arrows.
>
> **Perception Layer (1.5 marks):** Acquires physiological signals via biosensors. Converts physical/chemical quantities into electrical signals. **Example:** ECG electrodes, SpO₂ photodetector.
>
> **Network Layer (1.5 marks):** Transmits digitized data over wired/wireless links using protocols like BLE, ZigBee, Wi-Fi, NB-IoT, 5G.
>
> **Application Layer (1 mark):** Performs data analytics, alerting, and integration with EHR/clinical systems.

**Model Solution (b):**

**(i) Raw bit rate:**

$$
R_b = f_s \times n = 400 \times 10 = 4000 \text{ bps} = 4 \text{ kbps}
$$

**(ii) Bandwidth required (BPSK, M = 2):**

$$
BW = \frac{R_b}{\eta \log_2 M} = \frac{4000}{0.5 \times 1} = 8000 \text{ Hz} = 8 \text{ kHz}
$$

**(iii) Power consumption:**

$$
P = V \times I = 3 \times 4 \times 10^{-3} = 0.012 \text{ W} = 12 \text{ mW}
$$

**(iv) Battery life:**

$$
T_{life} = \frac{200 \text{ mAh}}{4 \text{ mA}} = 50 \text{ hours} = \frac{50}{24} \approx 2.08 \text{ days}
$$

> Applying 15% derating: $T_{life} \approx 2.08 \times 0.85 \approx 1.77$ days.

---

#### **Question B — 14 Marks** *(Alternative Choice)*

**[KTU University Exam — Sample 2024 Scheme]**
**(a) [7 Marks]** Discuss **five major applications of IoT in healthcare**, giving the communication technology used in each.

**(b) [7 Marks]** A hospital uses a LoRaWAN link to monitor a remote patient 10 km away. Transmit power = 14 dBm, transmit antenna gain = 2 dBi, receive antenna gain = 2 dBi, frequency = 868 MHz, receiver sensitivity = −137 dBm. Calculate: (i) wavelength, (ii) free-space path loss, and (iii) received power. Comment on link feasibility.

**Model Solution (a):**

| # | Application | Communication Technology |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM)** of vitals | BLE → Cellular (4G/5G) |
| 2 | **Telemedicine / Teleconsultation** | Video over 4G/5G / WebRTC |
| 3 | **Smart Hospital Asset Tracking** (beds, pumps, ventilators) | RFID + Wi-Fi |
| 4 | **Medication Adherence Monitoring** | NFC + GSM |
| 5 | **Chronic Disease Management (e.g., diabetes CGM)** | BLE to smartphone → Cloud |
| 6 | **Epidemic Outbreak Surveillance** (aggregated wearable data) | LoRaWAN + Cloud analytics |
| 7 | **Smart Ambulance with live vitals streaming** | 5G URLLC |

*(Any five well-explained applications with correct technologies = 7 marks: 1 mark each + 2 marks for clarity of explanation.)*

**Model Solution (b):**

**(i) Wavelength:**

$$
\lambda = \frac{c}{f} = \frac{3 \times 10^8}{868 \times 10^6} \approx 0.3456 \text{ m}
$$

**(ii) Free-space path loss at d = 10 km:**

$$
L_{fs} = 20 \log_{10}\!\left(\frac{4\pi d}{\lambda}\right) = 20 \log_{10}\!\left(\frac{4\pi \times 10000}{0.3456}\right)
$$

$$
L_{fs} = 20 \log_{10}(363{,}632) \approx 111.2 \text{ dB}
$$

**(iii) Received power (Friis):**

$$
P_{rx} = P_{tx} + G_t + G_r - L_{fs}
$$

$$
P_{rx} = 14 + 2 + 2 - 111.2 = -93.2 \text{ dBm}
$$

**Link Feasibility Comment:**
> Since $P_{rx} = -93.2$ dBm $>$ Receiver Sensitivity $= -137$ dBm, the link has a **link margin of 43.8 dB**, which is highly feasible. This shows LoRaWAN's excellent long-range capability for rural telemedicine.

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Callout — Where Students Lose Marks:**
> 1. **Skipping the block diagram** in 7-mark questions. *Always draw* a labeled diagram for IoT architecture questions — it carries **2–3 marks by itself**.
> 2. **Forgetting units** in numericals (kbps vs Mbps, mW vs W). Examiners deduct 0.5 marks per missing unit.
> 3. **Confusing IoT layers** — Perception is **sensing**, Network is **transmission**, Application is **clinical analytics**. Don't mix them.
> 4. **Not applying Nyquist** explicitly — state $f_s \geq 2 f_{max}$ *before* the calculation, not just plug numbers.
> 5. **Ignoring path loss in real-world comments** — examiners love a concluding statement on link feasibility or real-world relevance.

---

### 🧠 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

- ✅ IoT in healthcare = **sensors + communication + analytics + clinical action**.
- ✅ **Three layers:** Perception (sensors) → Network (transmission) → Application (clinical use).
- ✅ **Nyquist theorem** $f_s \geq 2 f_{max}$ is the starting point for *every* biomedical sampling problem.
- ✅ **Shannon capacity** $C = B \log_2(1 + S/N)$ defines the upper bound of medical data throughput.
- ✅ **BLE** for short-range wearables; **Wi-Fi/5G** for in-hospital high-bandwidth; **LoRa/NB-IoT** for long-range remote.
- ✅ **ECG bit rate formula** = $f_s \times n \times$ (no. of leads) × overhead.
- ✅ **Battery life** = capacity ÷ average current draw; always apply ~15% derating.
- ✅ **Friis equation** $P_{rx} = P_{tx} + G_t + G_r - L_{fs}$ is the *only* link-budget formula you need.
- ✅ **Latency budget** for critical care must stay **< 1 second** end-to-end.
- ✅ **Reliability target** in medical IoT is **99.99% (4-nines)**.
- ✅ **MICS band (402–405 MHz)** is reserved for implanted medical devices.
- ✅ Always include a **concluding real-world statement** in KTU answers — examiners reward application-level thinking.
- ✅ **MQTT** is the dominant lightweight IoT protocol for medical telemetry; **CoAP** is its constrained alternative.
- ✅ Security considerations: **HIPAA** (US) and **DPDP Act 2023** (India) compliance is mandatory in KTU viva questions on IoT ethics.
